episode_name: Battle of Huế / Tet urban battle
geographic_scope: City-level with immediate western, northwestern, and southern approaches around Huế, Thừa Thiên Province, South Vietnam
theater_description: Huế city proper (Citadel north of the Perfume River and the modern city to the south) plus directly contested approach zones west, northwest, and southwest used by reinforcing and blocking forces.
geometry_method_note: No formal polygon recovered. A defensible later polygon would include the Citadel, south-bank city blocks, Hue University, key church/refuge complexes, the western Triangle/La Chu approach area, and directly engaged approach corridors.
military_area_note: Military denominator includes combatants in the city and directly assigned approach forces that materially shaped the battle.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator excludes whole-province population and uses the urban population physically present in the exposed Huế theater, with scenario reductions for displacement.
split_reason: Urban battle with bounded civilian footprint, distinctive force mix, and unusually rich source coverage relative to other Vietnam War episodes.
quality_note: Good historical source coverage, but the civilian direct-death series remains contested and force-exposure denominators are partly model-generated.

geographic_scope: District/corridor-level siege complex around Khe Sanh Combat Base in northwestern Quảng Trị Province
theater_description: Marine base, surrounding hill outposts, and Route 9 relief corridor near the Laotian border.
geometry_method_note: Likely polygon around Khe Sanh Combat Base, surrounding heights, and main relief/approach corridors.
military_area_note: Would need separation between base garrison, hill outposts, and Operation Pegasus relief forces.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator should be very small and highly displacement-sensitive; nearby refugee flows matter.
split_reason: Siege/relief episode with a very different civilian footprint and combatant exposure pattern from urban Tet fighting.
quality_note: Episode proposed only; no full estimate completed in this bundle.

geographic_scope: City/provincial-capital siege in Bình Long Province
theater_description: An Lộc town and immediate siege perimeter along Highway 13.
geometry_method_note: Likely bounded urban/siege polygon around An Lộc and immediate relief corridor segments.
military_area_note: Needs daily ARVN garrison strength, reinforcement flows, and PAVN siege-force reconstruction.
civilian_area_note: Requires town population and evacuation chronology rather than province-wide denominator.
split_reason: Distinct 1972 conventional-siege episode with heavy artillery and air support but a much smaller civilian footprint than whole-war Vietnam.
quality_note: Episode proposed only; no full estimate completed in this bundle.

episode_name: Quảng Trị recapture / Second Battle of Quảng Trị
geographic_scope: Provincial-capital and corridor battle in Quảng Trị Province
theater_description: Quảng Trị city, citadel, and associated river/corridor battles during the South Vietnamese counteroffensive.
geometry_method_note: Would need city-core polygon plus river crossing and approach corridors tied to the counteroffensive.
military_area_note: Requires ARVN marine/airborne exposure reconstruction plus PAVN defenders and U.S. fire-support treatment.
civilian_area_note: Needs city/provincial population present after the earlier Easter Offensive displacement wave.
split_reason: A 1972 high-intensity conventional urban/corridor battle with different belligerent structure and much more Vietnamized force composition.
quality_note: Episode proposed only; no full estimate completed in this bundle.

geographic_scope: Strategic bombing window focused on Hanoi/Haiphong in North Vietnam
theater_description: Air campaign against northern transportation, air-defense, and infrastructure targets centered on the Hanoi-Haiphong complex.
geometry_method_note: Would need strike-target cluster polygon rather than a continuous front-line polygon.
military_area_note: Military denominator should include bomber/escort aircrews and North Vietnamese air-defense forces directly engaged.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator requires strike-zone population exposure rather than national population.
split_reason: Airpower-heavy episode with radically different geometry and victim-side attribution structure from ground battles.
quality_note: Episode proposed only; no full estimate completed in this bundle.

episode_name: Final Spring Offensive / Fall of Saigon window
geographic_scope: Capital-region collapse and final offensive window centered on Saigon
theater_description: The final offensive culminating in the capture of Saigon and the collapse of South Vietnam.
geometry_method_note: Would need capital-region polygon plus key approach corridors and evacuation nodes.
military_area_note: Requires collapsing ARVN force-presence estimates and PAVN offensive order of battle by phase.
civilian_area_note: Requires capital-region population present during mass flight, encirclement, and evacuation.
split_reason: Terminal regime-collapse episode with large refugee flows and rapidly changing force structure.
quality_note: Episode proposed only; no full estimate completed in this bundle.
